problemStatement: TypeScript functions encode behavioral specifications implicitly in their implementations, but there is no way to state and verify formal properties about them. Users should be able to annotate TypeScript functions with Pantagruel assertions and model-check those assertions against the function's actual behavior, bridging the gap between implementation and specification.
solutionSummary: Build a two-part system: (1) extend Pantagruel with the `over each` aggregate quantifier to handle common TS patterns like reduce/sum/min/max, and (2) build a TypeScript tool (ts2pant) that parses TS functions and their participating types, translates them into Pantagruel declarations and propositions, merges user-provided @pant assertions, and invokes `pant --check` for SMT verification. The function is the unit of translation: pure functions become rules with defining propositions; mutating functions become actions with pre/post conditions. Types referenced by the function are extracted as domains, type aliases, and rules.
currentStateAnalysis: Pantagruel is a mature spec language with lexer, parser, type checker, and SMT-based bounded model checker. It handles domains, rules, actions, quantifiers (all/some/each), conditionals, primed expressions, and function overrides. However: (1) it cannot express aggregation over collections (sum, min, max) — the quantifier family is incomplete, lacking combiners beyond and/or/collect; (2) there is no tooling to generate .pant files from source code in any language. The SMT backend already expands comprehensions over finite domain elements, so aggregate quantifiers can follow the same pattern with arithmetic combination.

mergabilityStrategy.featureFlagStrategy: No feature flags needed. Language extensions are additive (new syntax that doesn't affect existing specs). The translator is a new standalone tool.
mergabilityStrategy.patchOrderingStrategy: Patches 1-4 extend Pantagruel's language (INFRA — no existing behavior changes). Patches 5-6 set up the translator project structure (INFRA). Patches 7-10 implement translator features incrementally (BEHAVIOR). Patch 11 wires up end-to-end verification (BEHAVIOR).

requiredChanges.description: Add combiner type and extend EEach with optional combiner
requiredChanges.signature: type combiner = CombAdd | CombMul | CombAnd | CombOr | CombMin | CombMax

requiredChanges.description: Add combiner OVER EACH guards BAR expr production
Type-check aggregate expressions: verify body type matches combiner, infer result type
Encode aggregate quantifiers by expanding over domain elements and combining with arithmetic/logical operations
CLI entry point for ts2pant tool
TypeScript type and function extraction using TS compiler API
Translation from TS AST constructs to Pantagruel AST strings
Emit combined .pant file and invoke pant --check

acceptanceCriteria: Pantagruel parses and type-checks `+ over each x: T | e` syntax for all six combiners (+, *, and, or, min, max)
`pant --check` correctly verifies aggregate quantifier assertions via SMT
ts2pant extracts TypeScript interfaces, type aliases, and enums as Pantagruel domains and type aliases
ts2pant translates pure function signatures to Pantagruel rule declarations
ts2pant translates mutating function signatures to Pantagruel action declarations
ts2pant translates function bodies (conditionals, comparisons, arithmetic, property access) to Pantagruel propositions
ts2pant parses @pant JSDoc annotations and merges them with translated propositions
ts2pant generates a valid .pant file and invokes pant --check, reporting results
Existing Pantagruel tests continue to pass (language extension is additive)

explicitOpinions.opinion: The function is the unit of translation, not the module or class
explicitOpinions.rationale: A function's signature and body are self-contained enough to produce a meaningful spec. Module-level translation would require solving import resolution, re-exports, and other concerns that add complexity without proportional value.

explicitOpinions.opinion: over-each is a quantifier-level extension, not a library of built-in rules
explicitOpinions.rationale: sum/min/max over a domain cannot be defined in userspace because Pantagruel has no recursion or induction over list structure. The binding/iteration infrastructure already exists in quantifiers — the language just needs more combiners. The combiner goes before the quantifier (+ over each) to read naturally and parse unambiguously.

Every intermediate variable in a TS function can either be hoisted to its own Pantagruel rule or inlined into a single expression. Since the generated Pantagruel is an intermediate representation for SMT checking (not a human-maintained document), readability of the generated .pant is not a concern.

explicitOpinions.opinion: User assertions are the primary value; body translation is best-effort
explicitOpinions.rationale: The translator's main job is generating the structural context (domains, rules, action declarations) so users can write assertions without boilerplate. Body translation is useful but secondary — many TS patterns (async, exceptions, complex control flow) don't map cleanly to Pantagruel.

explicitOpinions.opinion: TypeScript compiler API over tree-sitter for parsing
explicitOpinions.rationale: We need resolved type information (what type is this property? what does this interface extend?), not just syntax. The TS compiler API provides the type checker, which is essential for mapping TS types to Pantagruel types.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Numeric type mapping must be a swappable strategy, not a hardcoded choice
explicitOpinions.rationale: TS `number` could reasonably map to Int, Real, or Nat0 depending on context. We don't yet know which default is best. The type mapper should be a configurable function (e.g. a strategy object or a CLI flag) so we can experiment without changing the core translation logic. @pant-type annotations provide per-parameter overrides regardless of default.

explicitOpinions.opinion: Body translation should be a separable, optional pass
explicitOpinions.rationale: We don't yet know how valuable body translation is vs skeleton-only. The architecture must allow the body translator to be toggled off entirely (e.g. --no-body flag) so users can rely purely on their @pant annotations. This means the emitter must work with zero translated propositions. Body translation can grow incrementally without blocking the core workflow.

patches.title: Add over-each AST, lexer tokens, and parser grammar

files.description: Add combiner variant type; change EEach to carry optional combiner
Add OVER, MIN, MAX tokens; reserve keywords
Add combiner rule and COMBINER OVER EACH production

changes: In ast.ml: add `type combiner = CombAdd | CombMul | CombAnd | CombOr | CombMin | CombMax [@@deriving show, eq]`
In ast.ml: change `EEach of param list * guard list * expr` to `EEach of param list * guard list * combiner option * expr`
In lexer.ml: add OVER, MIN, MAX to token definitions and keyword matching
In parser.mly: add OVER, MIN, MAX to token declarations
In parser.mly: add `combiner:` rule matching PLUS->CombAdd, TIMES->CombMul, AND->CombAnd, OR->CombOr, MIN->CombMin, MAX->CombMax
In parser.mly: add `| combiner OVER EACH quant_params_guards BAR expr` production yielding EEach with Some combiner
In parser.mly: update existing EACH production to yield EEach with None combiner
Update all EEach pattern matches in parser.mly helper functions (if any)

over-each > should parse 'max over each x: T, g | e' with guards

patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_PATCH_1.

> Patch 1 extends the AST and parser.
> The contribution is structural: new syntax is recognized.

Combiner.
Expression.

comb-add => Combiner.
comb-mul => Combiner.
comb-and => Combiner.
comb-or => Combiner.
comb-min => Combiner.
comb-max => Combiner.

has-over? e: Expression => Bool.
combiner e: Expression, has-over? e => Combiner.

---

> Every parsed over-each has a combiner.
all e: Expression, has-over? e | combiner e in Combiner.

For check.ml: pass through combiner without new type logic (Patch 3 adds that)
For smt.ml: when combiner is None, use existing comprehension logic unchanged; when Some, failwith 'not yet implemented' (Patch 4 adds encoding)
For pretty.ml: when combiner is Some, emit e.g. '+ over each ...' prefix
For markdown_output.ml: when combiner is Some, emit Unicode combiner symbol + 'over each' prefix
For json_output.ml: add 'combiner' field (null or string) to EEach serialization
For normalize.ml: pass combiner through unchanged

Expression.

patches.title: Type-check over-each aggregate expressions

files.description: Add type checking logic for aggregate combiners

changes: In the EEach type-checking branch: when combiner is None, use existing logic (body type T, result [T])
When combiner is Some CombAdd or CombMul: body must be numeric (Nat, Nat0, Int, or Real); result type = body type
When combiner is Some CombMin or CombMax: body must be numeric; result type = body type
When combiner is Some CombAnd or CombOr: body must be Bool; result type = Bool
Add error messages: 'aggregate combiner + requires numeric body, got T' etc.
Ensure existing each-without-combiner tests still pass
testStubsIntroduced: over-each typecheck > + over each with numeric body should return same numeric type
over-each typecheck > + over each with Bool body should be type error
over-each typecheck > and over each with Bool body should return Bool
over-each typecheck > and over each with numeric body should be type error
over-each typecheck > min over each with Nat body should return Nat
over-each typecheck > bare each behavior unchanged

over-each > should parse '+ over each x: T | e' as EEach with CombAdd
over-each > should parse 'max over each x: T, g | e' with guards

patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_PATCH_3.

> Type checking rules for aggregate quantifiers.

Combiner.
Type.

numeric? t: Type => Bool.

comb-add => Combiner.
comb-mul => Combiner.
comb-and => Combiner.
comb-or => Combiner.
comb-min => Combiner.
comb-max => Combiner.

bool => Type.

body-type c: Combiner => Type.
result-type c: Combiner, t: Type => Type.
requires-numeric? c: Combiner => Bool.
requires-bool? c: Combiner => Bool.

---

> Arithmetic combiners require numeric body.
all c: Combiner, requires-numeric? c |
  cond numeric? (body-type c) => result-type c (body-type c) = body-type c,
  true => false.

> Logical combiners require Bool body.
all c: Combiner, requires-bool? c |
  body-type c = bool.

requires-numeric? comb-add.
requires-numeric? comb-mul.
requires-numeric? comb-min.
requires-numeric? comb-max.
requires-bool? comb-and.
requires-bool? comb-or.

patches.title: SMT encoding for over-each aggregates

files.description: Implement SMT translation for aggregate quantifiers
Add SMT generation tests for over-each

changes: Add translate_aggregate function: expand comprehension over domain elements (reusing expand_comprehension), then combine with SMT operation
For CombAdd: combine expanded values with (+ v0 v1 ... vN), applying guards via ite (guarded ? value : 0)
For CombMul: combine with (* ...), identity 1
For CombMin: combine with nested (ite (< a b) a b), or use SMT theory min if available
For CombMax: combine with nested (ite (> a b) a b)
For CombAnd: combine with (and ...) — note this should produce same result as translate_quantifier forall
For CombOr: combine with (or ...) — same as exists
In translate_expr EEach branch: dispatch to translate_aggregate when combiner is Some
Handle empty domain case: return identity element for the combiner
Add SMT tests: generate queries for simple aggregate expressions and verify they produce expected SMT-LIB2
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_PATCH_4.

> SMT encoding: aggregates expand over finite domain elements.

DomainElement.
Combiner.

elements d: DomainElement => [DomainElement].

> The SMT encoding of an aggregate over a domain with N elements
> produces a combination of N values (one per element, guarded by
> the element's guard condition).

---

> For + combiner, unguarded elements contribute their value;
> guarded-out elements contribute the identity (0).
> Result: (+ (ite g0 v0 0) (ite g1 v1 0) ... (ite gN vN 0))
true.

Create 11-over-each.pant: a tutorial sample showing all six combiners with doc comments
Run pant --check on over-each-ok.pant to verify end-to-end SMT encoding works

patches.title: Extract TypeScript types as Pantagruel domains and rules

files.description: Use TS compiler API to extract interfaces, type aliases, enums, and their fields
Map TS type nodes to Pantagruel domain declarations, type aliases, and field-accessor rules
Tests for type extraction and translation

changes: In extract.ts: create a TS Program from a source file, walk the AST for InterfaceDeclaration, TypeAliasDeclaration, EnumDeclaration nodes
For each interface: extract name (-> PantDomain), properties (-> PantRule per property, e.g. `balance a: Account => Nat0`)
For type aliases: map `type Point = [number, number]` -> `Point = Int * Int.`; `type Result = Value | null` -> `Result = Value + Nothing.`
For enums: map to domain declaration

Map TS types via a NumericStrategy interface: { mapNumber(context): PantType }. Provide at least two implementations (IntStrategy, RealStrategy). Default selected by CLI flag --numeric-type (default: Int). string->String, boolean->Bool, null/undefined->Nothing, T[]->List, [A,B]->Product, A|B->Sum are fixed.
In translate-types.ts: produce PantDomain and PantRule arrays from extracted type info
Follow participating types: given a function, walk its parameter types and return type, recursively extracting all referenced interfaces/types
Tests: verify interface->domain+rules, type alias->alias, enum->domain, recursive type following
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_PATCH_7.

TSType.
PantDeclaration.

> Every TS interface produces a domain and one rule per property.
interface-fields t: TSType => [PantDeclaration].

---

> Interfaces have at least one declaration (the domain itself).
all t: TSType | #(interface-fields t) >= 1.

patches.title: Translate function signatures to rules or actions

files.description: Classify functions as pure/mutating and translate to rule or action declarations
Tests for signature translation

changes: Analyze function: pure (no assignments to parameters/properties, returns a value) -> PantRule; mutating (assigns to .prop, void return) -> PantAction
For pure functions: translate `function foo(a: Account, b: number): string` to `foo a: Account, b: Int => String.`
For mutating functions: translate `function deposit(a: Account, amount: number): void` to `~> Deposit @ a: Account, amount: Int.`
Handle methods: `account.withdraw(amount)` where `this` is the first parameter
For actions, detect guard patterns: `if (cond) { ... } else { throw }` -> declaration guard
Tests: pure function -> rule, void mutator -> action, guarded mutator -> action with guard
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_PATCH_8.

TSFunction.
PantDeclaration.

classification f: TSFunction => Kind.
pure => Kind.
mutating => Kind.

Kind.

translated f: TSFunction => PantDeclaration.

---

> Every function translates to exactly one declaration.
all f: TSFunction | translated f in PantDeclaration.

> Pure functions become rules; mutating functions become actions.
all f: TSFunction, classification f = pure | is-rule? (translated f).
all f: TSFunction, classification f = mutating | is-action? (translated f).

where

is-rule? d: PantDeclaration => Bool.
is-action? d: PantDeclaration => Bool.

---

patches.title: Translate function bodies to propositions

files.description: Translate TS expression patterns to Pantagruel propositions
Tests for body translation

changes: For pure functions: translate the return expression to a defining proposition `all params | f args = <translated return expr>`
Translate TS expression patterns: ternary -> cond, === -> =, !== -> !=, &&/|| -> and/or, ! -> ~, +/-/*// -> arithmetic ops
Translate property access: `a.balance` -> `balance a` (rule application)
Translate array operations: `.length` -> `#`, `.includes(x)` -> `x in`, `.filter(p).map(f)` -> `each x: T, p x | f x`
For mutating functions: translate each `obj.prop = val` to a primed proposition `prop' obj = val`
Generate frame conditions for actions: for each rule in scope not explicitly assigned, emit `all x: T | rule' x = rule x`
Translate conditionals in bodies: `if (g) return a; else return b` -> `cond g => a, true => b`
Handle unsupported patterns gracefully: emit a comment `> UNSUPPORTED: <pattern description>` and skip
Tests: return expression -> proposition, property access -> rule app, ternary -> cond, assignment -> primed, frame conditions generated
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_PATCH_9.

> Body translation: TS expressions become Pantagruel propositions.

TSExpression.
PantProposition.

supported? e: TSExpression => Bool.
translated e: TSExpression, supported? e => PantProposition.

---

> Supported expressions translate to propositions.
all e: TSExpression, supported? e |
  translated e in PantProposition.

patches.title: Parse @pant annotations from TypeScript comments

files.description: Extract @pant annotations from JSDoc comments preceding functions
Tests for annotation parsing

changes: Walk JSDoc comments attached to the target function node
Extract lines matching `@pant <proposition>` — each becomes a raw Pantagruel proposition string
Support multi-line annotations: @pant-begin / @pant-end blocks for complex propositions
Support @pant-type annotations for overriding TS->Pantagruel type mappings (e.g. `@pant-type amount: Nat` to override default Int)
Validate extracted propositions are non-empty strings (actual Pantagruel parsing happens when pant --check runs)
Tests: single @pant line, multiple @pant lines, multi-line block, @pant-type override, function with no annotations
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_PATCH_10.

Annotation.
TSFunction.

annotations f: TSFunction => [Annotation].
raw-text a: Annotation => String.
valid? a: Annotation => Bool.

---

> Every annotation has a validity status.
all a: Annotation | valid? a or ~valid? a.

patches.title: End-to-end: emit .pant and run pant --check

files.description: Assemble translated declarations + propositions + annotations into a .pant file and invoke pant --check
End-to-end tests with sample TypeScript functions
Sample: annotated mutating function
Sample: annotated pure function

changes: In emit.ts: assemble PantDocument from translated domains, rules, actions, propositions, and annotation strings
Generate module header: `module <FUNCTION_NAME>.`
Emit chapter head: all domain declarations, type aliases, rule declarations, action declarations
Emit `---` separator
Emit chapter body: translated propositions from function body, followed by user's @pant assertions
Write to temp file, invoke `pant --check <file>` via child_process.execSync
Parse pant --check output: report pass/fail per check, surface counterexamples

In index.ts: wire up the full pipeline: extract types -> translate signature -> (optionally) translate body -> parse annotations -> emit -> check. When --no-body is set, skip body translation entirely — emit only the structural skeleton + user annotations.
E2E test with deposit.ts fixture: `@pant all a: Account | balance a >= 0` should be checkable
E2E test with max.ts fixture: `@pant all a: Int, b: Int | max a b >= a and max a b >= b` should pass
patches.spec:
module TS2PANT_PATCH_11.

> End-to-end: TS function + annotations -> .pant -> pant --check.

TSFunction.
PantDocument.
CheckResult.

generated-doc f: TSFunction => PantDocument.
check-result f: TSFunction => CheckResult.

passed? r: CheckResult => Bool.

---

> Every function with annotations produces a checkable document.
all f: TSFunction, #(annotations f) > 0 |
  generated-doc f in PantDocument.

> Check result is determined by pant --check.
all f: TSFunction, #(annotations f) > 0 |
  check-result f in CheckResult.

where

annotations f: TSFunction => [Annotation].
Annotation.

---

testMap.testName: over-each > should lex OVER, MIN, MAX as distinct tokens
over-each > should parse '+ over each x: T | e' as EEach with CombAdd
over-each > should parse 'max over each x: T, g | e' with guards
over-each > should parse bare 'each x: T | e' as EEach with None combiner
over-each typecheck > + over each with numeric body should return same numeric type
over-each typecheck > + over each with Bool body should be type error
over-each typecheck > and over each with Bool body should return Bool
over-each typecheck > and over each with numeric body should be type error
over-each typecheck > min over each with Nat body should return Nat
over-each typecheck > bare each behavior unchanged

mergabilityInsight: 5 of 11 patches are INFRA. Patches 1-5 (Pantagruel language extension) and patches 6-11 (translator) form two independent tracks that can be developed in parallel until patch 11 joins them. The language extension is fully additive — no existing behavior changes.
finalStateSpec:
module TS2PANT.

> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> AGGREGATE QUANTIFIERS
> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> Pantagruel's quantifier family is extended with
> arithmetic combiners: COMBINER over each GUARDS | BODY.

Combiner.
Type.
Expression.

comb-add => Combiner.
comb-mul => Combiner.
comb-and => Combiner.
comb-or => Combiner.
comb-min => Combiner.
comb-max => Combiner.

has-over? e: Expression => Bool.
body-type e: Expression => Type.
result-type e: Expression => Type.
numeric? t: Type => Bool.
bool => Type.

---

> Arithmetic combiners require numeric body; result type = body type.
all e: Expression, has-over? e, numeric? (body-type e) |
  result-type e = body-type e.

where

> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> TRANSLATION PIPELINE
> ══════════════════════════════════════════
> Given a TypeScript function and its participating types,
> ts2pant produces a checkable Pantagruel document.

TSFunction.
TSType.
PantDocument.
PantDeclaration.
PantProposition.
Annotation.
CheckResult.

> Extraction: every referenced TS type becomes declarations.
referenced-types f: TSFunction => [TSType].
translated-types t: TSType => [PantDeclaration].

> Classification: pure functions -> rules, mutating -> actions.
pure? f: TSFunction => Bool.
mutating? f: TSFunction => Bool.

> Translation: function -> declaration + propositions.
translated-sig f: TSFunction => PantDeclaration.
translated-body f: TSFunction => [PantProposition].

> Annotations: user-provided assertions.
annotations f: TSFunction => [Annotation].

> Assembly: combined document.
generated-doc f: TSFunction => PantDocument.
check-result f: TSFunction => CheckResult.
passed? r: CheckResult => Bool.

---

> Every function is either pure or mutating.
all f: TSFunction | pure? f or mutating? f.
~(some f: TSFunction | pure? f and mutating? f).

> Every referenced type produces at least one declaration.
all t: TSType | #(translated-types t) >= 1.

> Every function with annotations produces a checkable document.
all f: TSFunction, #(annotations f) > 0 |
  generated-doc f in PantDocument.
